aboutMe: I am Divine Orji, a software engineer building thoughtful digital products through Kreativ and for teams that care about clarity, speed, and real user value.
Today, I serve as Chief Nerd at Kreativ, where I oversee tech operations globally, while continuing hands-on engineering work across product interfaces, frontend systems, and client delivery.

My recent product work includes leading the Crunchies Fried Chicken website redesign with Next.js, Tailwind CSS, and Google Maps integration so customers can choose delivery locations and find nearby outlets with less friction.

Alongside product engineering, I spent over three years at Hackmamba creating technical content, product demos, and community programs. I still document my thinking in public through my technical writing portfolio.

highlights.quote: Chief Nerd at Kreativ, overseeing tech operations globally while staying close to the work.
Software Engineer at Innovation Growth Hub, contributing to high-impact engineering projects.
Led the Crunchies redesign with Next.js, Tailwind CSS, and Google Maps-powered delivery flows.
Created technical content, demos, and hackathon programs during a 3+ year run at Hackmamba.

education.institution: Michael Okpara University of Agriculture Umudike

projects.title: Crunchies Online
description: I took the lead on this exciting project, where I completely redesigned the UI to make it more intuitive and visually appealing for users.
Using my skills in Next.js and Tailwind CSS, I built a responsive, fast-loading site that reflects my passion for performance optimization.

One of my favorite parts was integrating the Google Maps API—I added a feature that lets users pick their delivery location and find nearby Crunchies outlets with ease, enhancing the overall user experience.

projects.title: Merphils
description: Merphils is a unified education platform designed to connect school operations, family access, learner records, and future cross-school collaboration in one system.

The product is built around a single-account, context-aware identity model that lets one person move across multiple schools and roles such as owner, staff member, parent, or student without creating duplicate identities.

It is being shaped to support class management, assignments, grades, admissions, finance, and long-term academic history while keeping school data private by default and learner continuity intact over time.

projects.title: Grooovy
description: Grooovy is a nightlife and social event discovery platform built to surface the plans that usually stay buried in group chats, stories, and word of mouth.

The experience combines a public vibe-first discovery flow with guided onboarding, Supabase-backed authentication, and a protected dashboard where users can manage plans, tickets, wallet activity, and hosting tools.
Across public event pages, RSVP and checkout flows, host workspaces, and admin views, the product is being designed to make the path from curiosity to commitment feel warm, cinematic, and fast.

projects.title: Kadric Ltd
description: Kadric is an industrial supply and support company serving marine, offshore, oil and gas, and broader industrial operations with procurement, HSE products, technical sourcing, and engineering support.

The website is built to make complex supply capability easier to understand, with clear service categories, RFQ-driven inquiry flows, trust signals, and a stronger public story around reliability, safety, and delivery coordination.

From product discovery to quote requests, the experience is positioned around practical operational needs: speed, fit-for-purpose sourcing, and confidence that the supplier can respond under pressure.

projects.title: The Iroko Circle
description: The Iroko Circle is a literary community and editorial platform dedicated to reading, discussing, and amplifying African stories.

The website brings together essays, features, poetry, reviews, short stories, community join flows, chapter growth, submissions, and newsletter touchpoints in one clear publishing and community experience.
Its job is not just to publish content, but to make discovery, participation, and literary conversation feel welcoming for readers, writers, and supporters of African literature.

projects.title: The Bakistry
description: The Bakistry is a handcrafted cakes and treats brand built around fast delivery, premium presentation, and a warm storefront for everyday celebrations and special occasions.
The site combines category-led shopping, cart and checkout flows, featured products, and editorial content so customers can move easily from inspiration to order.
From Budget Express Cakes to custom gateaux, the experience is designed to balance appetite, speed, and visual delight without making the buying path feel heavy.

projects.title: The Developer Marketing Book
description: The Developer Marketing Book is a focused launch site for a book on reaching and engaging developers through strategy, community, and partnerships.
The site brings together the core value proposition, book formats, audiobook promotion, testimonials, and author credibility in one fast marketing surface.
Its structure is intentionally tight: help the right reader understand the promise quickly, browse proof, and take a clear next step toward purchase or listening.
